narrative.headline: 2020 Chevy Tahoe: electrical, power train, and engine lead owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 50 complaints with NHTSA. The electrical system is the most reported area at 22%, followed closely by the power train and engine, each at 20%. Brakes and steering round out the top five at 12% apiece. No fires were reported, but the steering complaints are the most serious: they account for 1 crash and 6 injuries.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The top complaint area: 11 reports (22%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but it is the most common owner concern, so check that warning lights and electronics behave normally.

issues.sample: The contact owns a 2020 Chevrolet Tahoe. The contact stated that while driving upwards of 80 MPH, the vehicle would violently shake without warning. The vehicle was not taken to be diagnosed or repaired. The manufacturer was not notified of the failure. The failure mileage was ap
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 10 reports (20%). No crashes, fires, or injuries tied to these, but test how the car drives and shifts at speed.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Tied for second: 10 reports (20%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported. Watch for any check-engine light or loss of power during a test drive.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 6 reports (12%). No crashes, fires, or injuries reported, but try the brakes and pedal feel for yourself before buying.

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Smaller in count but the most serious: 6 reports (12%), tied to 1 crash and 6 injuries. This is the one to inspect most carefully.
